response_parsed.opinion: When you find yourself in a discussion, whether it’s over the dinner table or scrolling through social media, that veers into the deep end of healthcare debates—say, concerning COVID vaccines or general medical practices—it can feel like navigating a minefield, really. Given my disposition, I tend to be quite curious and open-minded enough to want to understand *why* someone believes what they do, but I also know where my own boundaries lie, especially when the conversation starts getting heated or based purely on emotion rather than fact. Since I've spent a good amount of time traveling and observing different communities, I’ve learned that people often approach these topics from deeply personal places—their family health history, their beliefs, their fears.
